# fix(kaizen-observability): hash tenant_id before INFO/WARN emission (H1)

<!-- Generated by SessionEnd hook from a commit matching a journal-worthy pattern.
     Next session: review, then promote to a real journal entry OR delete. -->

**Commit**: `951848acd840` — fix(kaizen-observability): hash tenant_id before INFO/WARN emission (H1)

**Body**:

Raw tenant_id on WARN+ log lines bleeds schema-level identifiers into
broader-audience log aggregators (Datadog, Splunk, CloudWatch) per
rules/observability.md §8 and rules/tenant-isolation.md §4. Fix:
all five TraceExporter log sites + two AgentDiagnostics log sites now
route tenant_id through _hash_tenant_id(), producing the cross-SDK
sha256:<8-hex> prefix form that matches the payload_hash contract
(rules/event-payload-classification.md §2).

Forensic correlation stays stable (same raw -> same hash across
Python and Rust log streams); schema enumeration via log-aggregator
grep is no longer possible.

Closes the tenant-id regression test added in the previous commit
(test_tenant_id_hashed_not_raw_on_warn_plus_log_lines).

PR#6 of issue #567 — security-reviewer feedback on PR#587 (H1).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
